core_concept: Supply and Demand: How Prices Rise and Fall Based on Availability and Desire
facts: When a new toy is very popular but only a few are made, the toy's price often goes up because demand is high and supply is low.
A surplus happens when too many items are available and not enough people want to buy them, causing the price to drop.
Scarcity means something is limited in supply; for example, if only a small number of tickets are sold for a concert, those tickets become scarce and can be sold at a higher price.
Even if a seller wants to charge a high price, they must lower it when many other sellers offer the same item, because competition for buyers can force prices down.
misconceptions: If a store has many copies of a game, the game must be very expensive because there are so many available.
When a popular snack runs out quickly in stores, students think the price will go down because the store wants to get rid of the last ones.
